title: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
subhead: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

title: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter ceasefire
Giorgia Meloni and the defeat of ideology
Melania Trump blasts 'lies' linking her to Epstein: 'I am not his victim'
subhead: Jeffrey Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Donald Trump's second presidency. 'I was not a participant. Was never on Epstein's plane, and never visited his private island,' the first lady said.

title: In Budapest, a seven-hour concert aims to hasten Viktor Orban's 'end of the regime'
subhead: Robert Puzser, an intellectual and outspoken critic of the Fidesz party led by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, is organizing this historic free show on Friday, April 10, to urge citizens to turn out for Sunday's legislative elections.

title: 'Abstract Constructions' in Saint-Paul-de-Vence: The vibrant colors of Nassos Daphnis, from horticulture to abstract art
subhead: The American painter, who took part in the urban art movement in New York in the 1970s, had not been exhibited in France since 1962.

title: Three writers on how Simone de Beauvoir influenced their relationship with feminism
subhead: For the 40th anniversary of the philosopher's death, French writers Louise Chennevière, Pauline Delabroy-Allard and Blandine Rinkel share how Beauvoir has impacted them with Le Monde.
byline: Blandine Rinkel ,  Louise Chennevière and  Pauline Delabroy-Allard

Denis Charbit: 'The Israeli lawmakers who established the death penalty merely obeyed their own instincts for vengeance'
subhead: The professor at the Open University of Israel looks into the recent law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorist acts and anti-Israeli attacks. He sees it as a grave moral regression and is concerned about Israel abandoning 'thousands of years of ethics.'

title: Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
subhead: Members of Congress, stunned by the crude and genocidal language used by the president, have begun to discuss the US Constitution's 25th Amendment, which allows for the president to be declared unfit and for executive powers to be transferred to the vice president.

title: Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town
subhead: For the past three years in the village of Goudelin, someone has taken pleasure in harassing women living alone, often elderly, by pounding on their doors in the middle of the night. Around 30 victims have been identified. Speculation is rampant about the knocker's identity, and the victims are finally being heard.

title: War in Iran: Trump's elusive victory
'The US administration's dysfunctions and the Iranian regime's resilience are driving Trump further into an obsession with force'
